name: cand-fill-categories-and-spaces
notes: Strategy-only: reframes filling fields/crops/animals/stables as concrete time-boxed scoring actions and treats every empty category/space as a 2-point swing, countering the "do it later" deferral.

blocks.strategy:
Strategy basics: feed the family first (begging is -3 each), and grow the family as early as housing allows (each member = 3 points + an extra action). Beyond that, FILLING THE BOARD IS SCORING, not optional infrastructure. Every empty category is -1 and every unused farmyard space is -1, so turning one empty thing into a filled thing is a 2-point swing — treat it as urgent, not 'later'. Hit these concrete targets:
- Plow your first field by ~round 3-4 and SOW it (grain field holds 3 grain, veg field holds 2) — a plowed-but-unsown field still scores nothing for grain/veg. Get a vegetable into a field by end-game so Vegetables isn't -1 (Vegetable Seeds opens stage 3; one grabbed veg sown gives you the category).
- Start at least one animal type early: Sheep Market is open from round 1 and dumps its whole pile to one placement. Keep two animal types alive to the end so two of Sheep/Boar/Cattle aren't -1; a breeding pair (2 of a type) makes a free offspring each harvest.
- Build a first pasture by ~round 6-7 (fences are 1 wood each) and an unfenced stable is cheap (2 wood) — both convert an empty space into a USED space (removing -1), and a stable inside a pasture is +1 and doubles capacity.
- Before grabbing more raw wood/clay, ask: can a placement THIS round turn resources I already hold into a field, stable, pasture, or sown crop? Don't let wood pile up unspent. Accumulation spaces hand over their entire pile in one grab, so one late placement can flip a -1 to +1.
- Aim for at most ~3 empty farmyard spaces by round 11; late game, spend spare wood on stables and fences to fill the rest. Don't leave whole categories at -1; convert spare resources into points.
